name: Indian Oil Corporation
oneLine: admin.iocl.com exposed in CT; Sucuri WAF in front
findings: admin.iocl.com publicly visible in CT logs
Strict SPF (-all), DMARC p=quarantine — solid email auth

fingerprints.stack: Sucuri WAF + GoDaddy TLS + ASP.NET Core
eolFlags: Cert rotation in 45 days; Sucuri version not disclosed
Admin path publicly enumerated in CT logs

attackPaths.name: TLS rotation interception + subdomain takeover
attackPaths.entry: Monitor WHOIS + CT for June 2026 renewal; submit unauthorised CSR or DNS hijack via registrar compromise.
attackPaths.pivot: Valid TLS cert allows MITM on admin.iocl.com traffic through Sucuri WAF.
attackPaths.objective: Intercept admin tokens; compromise dealer portal; exfiltrate customer data.

attackPaths.name: F5 BIG-IP CVE-2025-53521 (if APM deployed in backend)
attackPaths.entry: If F5 BIG-IP APM in stack: pre-auth RCE.
attackPaths.pivot: RCE on F5 → extract auth tokens → cPanel admin compromise.
attackPaths.objective: Arbitrary command execution; alter dealer locator; inject malware.

attackPaths.name: Dealer portal SQL injection via locator.iocl.com
attackPaths.entry: Fuzz /locator/search?location=X for SQLi on unparameterised queries.
attackPaths.pivot: Extract dealer DB creds, payment data via MSSQL backend.
attackPaths.objective: Credential harvest from dealer network; supply-chain intel.

attackPaths.name: Email spoofing + executive phishing
attackPaths.entry: iocl.com SPF tight (-all); admin subdomain may lack SPF.
attackPaths.pivot: Phishing from admin@iocl.com → CEO → procurement VPN, financials.

paths.preAi: TLS rotation = known attack window (cf. Triton/TRISIS cert abuse)
paths.mythos: Pre-registration of attacker domain + DNS hijack during renewal — high-confidence state-level pattern
paths.factor: ~3× if registrar compromised; detection negligible if Let's Encrypt race condition

paths.preAi: Standard OWASP A1 SQLi on geospatial queries
paths.mythos: Dealer networks = soft underbelly; SQLi → credential harvest → lateral via dealer workstation (cf. Colonial Pipeline 2021)

cisoPatchList.title: TLS rotation procedure audit + DNSSEC + CT monitoring
cisoPatchList.fix: Certificate pinning via HPKP. Enable DNSSEC. MFA-only registrar account. CT monitoring (certspotter). Rotate GoDaddy API keys.

cisoPatchList.title: Restrict admin.iocl.com — IP allowlist + 2FA
cisoPatchList.fix: Move admin off public DNS or IP-allowlist on WHM ports 2087/2083. Enforce 2FA. Key-based SSH only. Rotate admin SSH keys.

cisoPatchList.title: Dealer locator SQLi remediation
cisoPatchList.fix: Parameterise queries. Allowlist input validation. Sucuri WAF SQLi rules. Rate-limit /locator/search.

cisoPatchList.title: Email spoofing prevention — SPF on admin subdomain + DMARC p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: Add explicit SPF for admin.iocl.com. DMARC parent policy p=reject. DKIM keys.
cisoPatchList.validation: mxtoolbox SPF/DMARC audit; spoofed mail bounces

cisoPatchList.title: WordPress plugin security on news.iocl.com / wowstories.iocl.com
cisoPatchList.fix: Audit plugins. King Addons → 51.1.35+. Admin-only registration. WAF block on /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php admin payloads.

cisoPatchList.title: HTTP security headers verification across iocl.com surface
cisoPatchList.fix: All 6 headers; OWASP ZAP scan; remove server banner.

phase2.headlineQuestion: Given the June 2026 TLS rotation window and admin.iocl.com publicly enumerated in CT, what controls prevent DNS hijack or unauthorised CSR submission during renewal — and has the GoDaddy registrar account been audited for compromise?
